Fix per-surface, verifying the chunk manifest after each.
findings.affectedSurfaces: AdminDashboard (staff frontend) first paint — the highest-traffic staff page

findings.title: appointmentDepositBatch.js is both statically imported (3 files) and dynamically imported (13+ sites) — every `await import()` is a no-op split (INEFFECTIVE_DYNAMIC_IMPORT) adding runtime awaits and masking real build warnings
findings.evidence: Static importers: AdminDashboard.jsx:92 `import { createDepositBookingPair, provisionOpdLinkForBookingPair } from '../lib/appointmentDepositBatch.js';`, AppointmentFormModal.jsx:71 `import { createDepositBookingPair, provisionOpdLinkForBookingPair } from '../../lib/appointmentDepositBatch.js';`, DepositPanel.jsx:36 (cancelDepositBookingPair, deleteDepositBookingPair, provisionOpdLinkForBookingPair). Dynamic importers of the SAME module: AdminDashboard.jsx:3330, 3709, 4245, 4342, 4456, 4534, 5512, 6328; AppointmentFormModal.jsx:795, 851, 898, 947; AppointmentCalendarView.jsx:1303 — e.g. `const mod = await import('../lib/appointmentDepositBatch.js');`. Because the module is in the static graph of the same chunks, Rolldown cannot split it (build warns INEFFECTIVE_DYNAMIC_IMPORT) and the dynamic form only adds a needless promise/microtask per call. Making the statics dynamic instead would win nothing: the module is 63,780 bytes and its deps (firebase.js, branchSelection) already load with the entry.
findings.suggestedFix: Unify on the static form: replace the 13 `await import('.../appointmentDepositBatch.js')` call sites with the already-imported static named bindings (module identity is unchanged — provably safe, no behavior difference). Kills the INEFFECTIVE_DYNAMIC_IMPORT warning so future genuine split regressions stay visible.
findings.affectedSurfaces: AdminDashboard + AppointmentFormModal + AppointmentCalendarView deposit-booking handlers (micro runtime cost); build-log signal quality

findings.title: server.warmup lists three V50-DELETED files (AppointmentTab.jsx, MasterDataTab.jsx, CloneTab.jsx) — dead warmup entries that error/no-op on every dev-server start
findings.evidence: vite.config.js:75 `'./src/components/backend/AppointmentTab.jsx',`, :78 `'./src/components/backend/MasterDataTab.jsx',`, :80 `'./src/components/backend/CloneTab.jsx',` — all three files verified nonexistent (`ls` → No such file or directory; deleted in V50, 2026-05-08, per BackendDashboard.jsx:32/:37 comments "CloneTab DELETED"/"MasterDataTab DELETED"). The warmup list also predates the current tab set (e.g. no reports/recall entries), so the dev cold-start pre-compile budget is spent on the wrong files.
findings.suggestedFix: Delete the three dead lines; optionally replace with the current heaviest real entry points (e.g. './src/components/backend/AppointmentCalendarView.jsx', './src/components/TreatmentFormPage.jsx'). Dev-only, zero prod effect.
findings.affectedSurfaces: Dev server cold start only (no production impact)

findings.title: PatientForm (116KB src) and PrintTemplates (41KB src) are statically imported in App.jsx while all six sibling pages are lazy — they inflate the shared entry chunk (365KB/87KB gz) paid by staff/backend routes too
findings.evidence: App.jsx:23 `import { OfficialOPDPrint, DashboardOPDPrint } from './components/PrintTemplates.jsx';`, :25 `import PatientForm from './pages/PatientForm.jsx';` (116,402 + 41,534 bytes src) versus :28-33 where AdminDashboard/PatientDashboard/ClinicSchedule/BackendDashboard/TabletChartEditorPage/FillerSimulator are all `lazy(() => import(...))`. dist entry index-CM1i0cpP.js = 365.3KB raw / 87.1KB gz (bundle-baseline.json) and is the `<script type=module>` in dist/index.html:33 — every route, including the staff dashboards that never render PatientForm, parses it at first paint. CAUTION: eager PatientForm may be a deliberate reliability choice — the 2026-06-16 mobile-load-reliability fix hardened patient QR links against stalled lazy-chunk fetches; lazifying PatientForm adds one chunk fetch back onto that mobile-critical path.
findings.suggestedFix: Measure-first candidate, only AFTER the recall-chunk fix lands: if entry size still matters, lazy-load PatientForm + PrintTemplates with the same LoadErrorRetry-guarded pattern the other pages use, and L1-verify patient-QR first-load on a throttled mobile profile (Rule Q) before shipping. If the mobile-reliability tradeoff is judged not worth it, leave as-is (documented intentional).
findings.affectedSurfaces: Every route's entry download/parse; primarily wasted on AdminDashboard/BackendDashboard sessions
